title: sorbus aria subsp. lanifera
intro: Sorbus aria subsp. lanifera, also known as the woolly whitebeam, is a subspecies of the whitebeam tree, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Sorbus aria subsp. lanifera include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves or bark in hot water to create teas, tinctures, or infusions that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
